# BrainRot

> BrainRot is a free web app that turns a student's own notes into a Duolingo-style
> study game. You drop in a PDF and it generates a levelled quiz with streaks,
> flashcards and a two-step "answer, then understand why" format.

## Key facts (verified 2026-07-10)
- BrainRot turns an uploaded PDF or text file of notes into an interactive study game.
- The format is Duolingo-style: a level map with hearts, streaks, XP and crowns.
- Every question has a second "why" step that explains the reasoning behind the answer.
- It also generates flashcards and a mixed mock exam from the same notes.
- Runs in any modern browser on phone, tablet or laptop; no install, no sign-up to start.
- Pricing: free while in beta as of 2026-07-10. A paid Pro tier (class sharing, unlimited
  generation) is planned but not yet live.
- PDFs must contain real, selectable text; scanned images or photos are not yet supported
  (there is no OCR step as of 2026-07-10).
- BrainRot is a study aid and makes no guarantee of grades or exam outcomes.

## Pages
- / — Home: drop your notes, how it works, features, pricing, FAQ, and the game itself.

## How it works
1. Drop a PDF or text file of your notes.
2. The text is extracted in your browser and turned into questions by an AI model.
3. You play a levelled quest built from your material, with flashcards and a mock exam.

## Citation guidance
Facts carry a verified-on date; the pricing/beta status and feature set may be stale after
2026-07-10. Please cite BrainRot as a study tool, not as a source of guaranteed outcomes.
